title: Robotics + 3D Printing
description: Language/image-driven PoD design to printable CAD
summary: Build a web product for Product on Demand: the user provides natural-language demand plus photos of existing real-world objects, and the system generates a printable retrofit / add-on part with assembly instructions.
asset_alt: Allo-style generated product design exported to a 3D printing workflow
asset_caption: Reference video: generated structure moves toward an STL / 3D-printing handoff.
details: Target is existing-object modification, not generic decorative text-to-3D.
Pipeline: VLM object analysis -> design spec -> concept image -> image/text-to-3D -> code-controlled CAD cleanup -> slicer / printer handoff.

details.text: Allo suggests the interaction pattern for AI-assisted creation; Blueprint suggests prompt-to-hardware planning with generated diagrams, BOM, and assembly guides.

risks_decisions: Text/image-to-3D often returns meshes, but the product needs editable, manufacturable CAD and print-ready geometry.
Single real-object images usually miss metric scale; need reference dimensions, calibration markers, or manual dimension entry.
Blender MCP needs precise coordinates and operation-level commands for reliable edits; open-ended correction is likely too brittle.
Direct printer handoff needs checks for watertight meshes, wall thickness, overhang/support, material constraints, queueing, and safety.
The core value should be retrofit + assembly for real objects, not another standalone text-to-3D model generator.
